caption: Canadian scientists Katherine Stewart and Diane Haughland and their graduate students meet us in the field for a CrustNet installation at Grasslands National Park, Saskatchewan, Canada.
The field crew hikes to the Lubrecht Experimental Forest CrustNet site on a rainy day carrying materials for installing rainfall reduction shelters.
Water infiltration measurement on a permanent biocrust plot at Lubrecht Experimental Forest.
USDA scientist, Rory O'Connor, joins the field crew for the CrustNet installation at the Northern Great Basin Experimental Range in Oregon, USA.
A field crew takes functional measurements on a permanent plot under a rainfall reduction shelter at MPG Ranch in Montana, USA.
Implementing disturbance treatments at Grasslands National Park.
Sampling at Jornada Experimental Range, New Mexico, USA.

Identifying Tier 2 plots in Idaho, USA (NAF node).

Julieta Aranibar and postdoc, Vanessa, check biocrust vouchers at Ñacuñan Biosphere Reserve (NBR node).
Matthew Bowker shows a flake of soil stabilized by biological soil crust at Ñacuñan Biosphere Reserve (NBR node).
